heading: Free Application for Federal Student Aid
label: 1090 Free Application for Federal Student Aid
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (a)(2)(B)(ii)(XVII)(aa), (dd), (ff), is , . Part A of title IV of the Act is classified generally to part A (§ 601 et seq.) of subchapter IV of chapter 7 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. Title XVI of the Act is classified generally to subchapter XVI (§ 1381 et seq.) of chapter 7 of Title 42. Title XIX of the Act is classified generally to subchapter XIX (§ 1396 et seq.) of chapter 7 of Title 42. Identification of Foster Youth on FAFSA, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–235, div. G, title III, § 308128 Stat. 2504Dec. 16, 2014“The Secretary of Education shall—“(1)modify the Free Application for Federal Student Aid described in section 483 of the HEA [Higher Education Act of 1965, ] so that the Free Application for Federal Student Aid contains an individual box for the purpose of identifying students who are foster youth or were in the foster care system; and20 U.S.C. 1090“(2)utilize such identification as a tool to notify students who are foster youth or were in the foster care system of their potential eligibility for Federal student aid, including postsecondary education programs through the John H. Chafee Foster Care Independence Program and any other Federal programs under which such students may be eligible to receive assistance.”Similar provisions were contained in the following prior appropriation act:, , .Pub. L. 113–76, div. H, title III, § 310128 Stat. 400Jan. 17, 2014

L. 111–39, title IV, § 407(a)123 Stat. 1950July 1, 2009“Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Education shall be required to carry out the requirements under the following provisions of section 483 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 () only for academic year 2010–2011 and subsequent academic years:20 U.S.C. 1090“(1)In subsection (a) of such section—“(A)subparagraphs (A)(i) and (B) of paragraph (2);“(B)in paragraph (3)—“(i)the second sentence of subparagraph (A);“(ii)clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (B); and“(iii)subparagraph (C);“(C)paragraph (4)(A)(iv); and“(D)paragraph (5)(E).“(2)Subsection (h) of such section.”

L. 113–76, div. H, title III, § 310128 Stat. 400Jan. 17, 2014

Delayed Implementation of EZ FAFSA, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–39, title IV, § 407(a)123 Stat. 1950July 1, 2009“Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Education shall be required to carry out the requirements under the following provisions of section 483 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 () only for academic year 2010–2011 and subsequent academic years:20 U.S.C. 1090“(1)In subsection (a) of such section—“(A)subparagraphs (A)(i) and (B) of paragraph (2);“(B)in paragraph (3)—“(i)the second sentence of subparagraph (A);“(ii)clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (B); and“(iii)subparagraph (C);“(C)paragraph (4)(A)(iv); and“(D)paragraph (5)(E).“(2)Subsection (h) of such section.”

children.heading: Simplified application for Federal student financial aid
children.label: a Simplified application for Federal student financial aid

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each individual seeking to apply for Federal financial aid under this subchapter for award year 2024–2025 and any subsequent award year shall file a free application with the Secretary, known as the “Free Application for Federal Student Aid”, to determine eligibility for such aid, as described in paragraph (2), and in accordance with .section 1087ss of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make available, for the purposes of paragraph (1), a free application to determine the eligibility of a student for Federal financial aid under this subchapter.

children.heading: Information required by the applicant
children.label: B Information required by the applicant

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The applicant, and, if necessary, the parents or spouse of the applicant, shall provide the Secretary with the applicable information described in clause (ii) in order to be eligible for Federal financial aid under this subchapter.

children.label: I
children.text: Name.

children.label: II
children.text: Contact information, including address, phone number, email address, or other electronic address.

children.label: III
children.text: Social security number.

children.label: IV
children.text: Date of birth.

children.label: V
children.text: Marital status.

children.label: VI
children.text: Citizenship status, including alien registration number, if applicable.

children.label: VII
children.text: Sex.

children.label: VIII
children.text: Race or ethnicity, using categories developed in consultation with the Bureau of the Census and the Director of the Institute of Education Sciences that, to the greatest extent practicable, separately capture the racial groups specified in the American Community Survey of the Bureau of the Census.

children.label: IX
children.text: State of legal residence and date of residency.

children.label: aa
children.text: Name and location of the high school from which the applicant received, or will receive prior to the period of enrollment for which aid is sought, a regular high school diploma;

children.label: bb
children.text: name and location of the entity from which the applicant received, or will receive prior to the period of enrollment for which aid is sought, a recognized equivalent of a regular high school diploma; or

children.label: cc
children.text: if the applicant completed or will complete prior to the period of enrollment for which aid is sought, a secondary school education in a home school setting that is treated as a home school or private school under State law.

children.label: XI
children.text: Name of each institution where the applicant intends to apply for enrollment or continue enrollment.

children.label: XII
children.text: Year in school for period of enrollment for which aid is sought, including whether applicant will have finished first bachelor’s degree prior to the period of enrollment for which aid is sought.

children.label: XIII
children.text: Whether one or both of the applicant’s parents attended college.

children.label: aa
children.text: the annual amount of child support received, if applicable; and

children.label: bb
children.text: all required asset information not described in item (aa).

children.label: XV
children.text: The number of members of the applicant’s family who will also be enrolled in an eligible institution of higher education on at least a half-time basis during the same enrollment period as the applicant.

children.label: aa
children.text: Is an unaccompanied homeless youth, or is unaccompanied, at risk of homelessness, and self-supporting.

children.label: bb
children.text: Is an emancipated minor.

children.label: cc
children.text: Is in legal guardianship.

children.label: dd
children.text: Has been a dependent ward of the court at any time since the applicant turned 13.

children.label: ee
children.text: Has been in foster care at any time since the applicant turned 13.

children.label: ff
children.text: Both parents have died since the applicant turned 13.

children.label: gg
children.text: Is a veteran of the Armed Forces of the United States or is serving (on the date of the application) on active duty in the Armed Forces for other than training purposes.

children.label: hh
children.text: Is under the age of 24 and has a dependent child or relative.

children.label: ii
children.text: Does not have access to parental information due to an unusual circumstance described in .section 1087vv(d)(9) of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: The supplemental security income program under title XVI of the Social Security Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 1381

children.label: bb
children.text: The supplemental nutrition assistance program under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.), a nutrition assistance program carried out under section 19 of such Act (), or a supplemental nutrition assistance program carried out under .7 U.S.C. 20117 U.S.C. 2028section 1841(c) of title 48

children.label: cc
children.text: The free and reduced price school lunch program established under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 1751

children.label: dd
children.text: The program of block grants for States for temporary assistance for needy families established under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 601

children.label: ee
children.text: The special supplemental nutrition program for women, infants, and children established by .section 1786 of title 42

children.label: ff
children.text: The Medicaid program under title XIX of the Social Security Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 1396

children.label: gg
children.text: Federal housing assistance programs, including tenant-based assistance under section 1437f() of title 42, and public housing, as defined in .osection 1437a(b)(1) of title 42

children.label: hh
children.text: Refundable credit for coverage under a qualified health plan under .section 36B of title 26

children.label: ii
children.text: The Earned Income Tax Credit under .section 32 of title 26

children.label: jj
children.text: Any other means-tested program determined by the Secretary to be appropriate.

children.label: XVIII
children.text: If the applicant, or, if necessary, the parents or spouse of the applicant, reported receiving tax exempt payments from an individual retirement plan (as defined in ) distribution or from pensions or annuities on a Federal tax return, information as to how much of the individual retirement plan distribution or pension or annuity disbursement was a qualified rollover.section 7701 of title 26

children.label: XIX
children.text: If the applicant, or, if necessary, the parents or spouse of the applicant, reported receiving foreign income that is exempt from Federal taxation or for which a permanent resident of the United States or United States citizen receives a foreign tax credit, information regarding the amount of such foreign income.

children.label: XX
children.text: If the applicant, or, if applicable, the parents or spouse of the applicant, elects to report receiving college grant and scholarship aid included in gross income on a Federal tax return described in , information regarding the amount of such aidsection 1087vv(e)(2) of this title1So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

children.heading: Prohibition against requesting information more than once
children.label: iii Prohibition against requesting information more than once
children.text: Any information requested during the process of creating an account for completing the free application under this subsection, shall, to the fullest extent possible, not be required a second time for the same award year, or in a duplicative manner, when completing such free application except in the case of an unusual situation, such as a temporary inability to access an account for completing such free application.

children.heading: Change in family size
children.label: iv Change in family size
children.text: The Secretary shall provide a process by which an applicant shall confirm the accuracy of family size or update the family size with respect to such applicant for purposes of determining the need of such applicant for financial assistance under this subchapter based on a change in family size from the tax year data used for such determination.

children.heading: Single question for homeless status
children.label: v Single question for homeless status
I
children.text: on the form developed under this section for which the information is applicable, there is a single, easily understood screening question to identify an applicant who is an unaccompanied homeless youth or is unaccompanied, at risk of homelessness, and self-supporting; and

children.label: II
children.text: such question is distinct from those relating to an individual who does not have access to parental income due to an unusual circumstance.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: vi Adjustments
children.text: The Secretary shall disclose on the FAFSA that the student may, on a case-by-case basis, qualify for an adjustment under to the cost of attendance or the values of the data items required to calculate the student’s eligibility for a Federal Pell Grant or the student aid index for the student or parent.section 1087tt of this title

children.heading: Notification and approval of request for tax return information
children.label: C Notification and approval of request for tax return information
children.text: The Secretary shall notify students and borrowers who wish to submit an application for Federal student financial aid under this subchapter (as well as parents and spouses who must sign such an application or request or a Master Promissory Note on behalf of those students and borrowers) of the authority of the Secretary to require that such persons affirmatively approve that the Internal Revenue Service disclose their tax return information as described in .section 1098h of this title

children.heading: Authorizations available to the applicant
children.label: D Authorizations available to the applicant

children.heading: Authorization to disclose FAFSA information, including a redisclosure of tax return information, to institution, State higher education agency, and designated scholarship organizations
children.label: i Authorization to disclose FAFSA information, including a redisclosure of tax return information, to institution, State higher education agency, and designated scholarship organizations
I
children.text: Information described under section 6103()(13) of title 26.l

children.label: II
children.text: All information provided by the applicant on the application described by this subsection to determine the applicant’s eligibility for Federal financial aid under this subchapter and for the application, award, and administration of such Federal financial aid, except the name of an institution to which an applicant selects to redisclose information shall not be disclosed to any other institution.

children.heading: Authorization to disclose to benefits programs
children.label: ii Authorization to disclose to benefits programs
children.text: An applicant and, if necessary, the parents or spouse of the applicant may provide the Secretary with authorization to disclose to applicable agencies that handle applications for means-tested Federal benefit programs, as defined in , all information provided by the applicant on the application described by this subsection as well as such applicant’s student aid index and scheduled Federal Pell Grant award to assist in identification, outreach and application efforts for the application, award, and administration of such means-tested Federal benefits programs, except such information shall not include Federal tax information as specified in section 6103()(13)(C) of title 26.section 1087ss(b)(4)(H) of this titlel

children.label: i
children.text: an application under this section, the Secretary shall, as soon as practicable, perform the necessary functions with the Commissioner of Internal Revenue to calculate the applicant’s student aid index and scheduled award for a Federal Pell Grant, if applicable, assuming full-time enrollment for an academic year, and note to the applicant the assumptions relationship to the scheduled award; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an authorization under subparagraph (D), the Secretary shall, as soon as practicable, disclose the information described under such subparagraph, as specified by the applicant, in order for the applicant’s eligibility for Federal, State, or institutional student financial aid programs or means-tested Federal benefit programs to be estimated or determined.

children.heading: Work study wages
children.label: F Work study wages
children.text: With respect to an applicant who has received income earned from work under part C of this subchapter, the Secretary shall take the steps necessary to collect information on the amount of such income for the purposes of calculating such applicant’s student aid index and scheduled award for a Federal Pell Grant, if applicable, without adding additional questions to the FAFSA, including by collecting such information from institutions of higher education participating in work-study programs under part C of this subchapter.

children.heading: Information to be supplied by the Secretary of Education
children.label: 3 Information to be supplied by the Secretary of Education
i
children.text: The estimated dollar amount of a Federal Pell Grant scheduled award for which the applicant is eligible for such award year.

children.label: ii
children.text: Information on other types of Federal financial aid for which the applicant may be eligible (including situations in which the applicant could qualify for 150 percent of a scheduled Federal Pell Grant award and loans made under this subchapter) and how the applicant can find additional information regarding such aid.

children.label: aa
children.text: Net price by the income categories, as described under , and disaggregated by undergraduate and graduate programs, as applicable.section 1015a(i)(6) of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: Graduation rate.

children.label: cc
children.text: Retention rate.

children.label: dd
children.text: Transfer rate, if available.

children.label: II
children.text: Median debt of students upon completion.

children.label: III
children.text: Institutional default rate, as calculated under .section 1085 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: If the student is eligible for a student aid index of less than or equal to zero under , a notification of the Federal means-tested benefits that they have not already indicated they receive, but for which they may be eligible, and relevant links and information on how to apply for such benefits.section 1087mm of this title

children.label: v
children.text: Information on education tax benefits described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of or other applicable education tax benefits determined in consultation with the Secretary of the Treasury.section 25A(a) of title 26

children.label: vi
children.text: If the individual identified as a veteran, or as serving (on the date of the application) on active duty in the Armed Forces for other than training purposes, information on benefits administered by the Department of Veteran Affairs or Department of Defense, respectively.

children.label: vii
children.text: If applicable, the applicant’s current outstanding balance of loans under this subchapter.

children.heading: Information provided to the State
children.label: B Information provided to the State

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall redisclose, with authorization from the applicant in accordance with paragraph (2)(D)(i), to a State higher education agency administering State-based financial aid and serving the applicant’s State of residence, the information described under section 6103()(13) of title 26 and information described in paragraph (2)(B) for the application, award, and administration of grants and other student financial aid provided directly from the State to be determined by such State. Such information shall include the list of institutions provided by the applicant on the application.l

children.label: I
children.text: shall use the information provided under clause (i) solely for the application, award, and administration of State-based financial aid for which the applicant is eligible;

children.label: II
children.text: may use the information, except for the information described under section 6103()(13) of title 26, for State agency research that does not release any individually identifiable information on any applicant to promote college attendance, persistence, and completion;l

children.label: III
children.text: may use identifying information provided by student applicants on the FAFSA to determine whether or not a graduating secondary student has filed the application in coordination with local educational agencies or secondary schools to encourage students to complete the application; and

children.label: IV
children.text: may share the application information, excluding the information described under section 6103()(13) of title 26, with any other entity, only if such applicant provides explicit written consent of the applicant, except as provided in subclause (III).l

children.heading: Limitation on consent process
children.label: iii Limitation on consent process
children.text: A State may provide a consent process whereby an applicant may elect to share the information described in clause (i), except for the information described in section 6103()(13) of title 26, through explicit written consent to Federal, State, or local government agencies or tribal organizations to assist such applicant in applying for and receiving Federal, State, or local government assistance, or tribal assistance for any component of the applicant’s cost of attendance that may include financial assistance or non-monetary assistance.l

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: iv Prohibition
children.text: Any entity that receives applicant information under clause (iii) shall not sell, share, or otherwise use applicant information other than for the purposes outlined in clause (iii).

children.heading: Use of information provided to the institution
children.label: C Use of information provided to the institution
i
children.text: shall use the information provided to it solely for the application, award, and administration of financial aid to the applicant;

children.label: ii
children.text: may use the information provided, excluding the information described under section 6013()(13) of title 26, for research that does not release any individually identifiable information on any applicant, to promote college attendance, persistence, and completion; andl4So in original. Probably should be “section 6103()(13)”.4l

children.label: iii
children.text: shall not share such educational record information with any other entity without the explicit written consent of the applicant.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: D Prohibition
children.text: Any entity that receives applicant information under subparagraph (C)(iii) shall not sell, share, or otherwise use applicant information other than for the purposes outlined in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: FAFSA information that includes tax return information
children.label: E FAFSA information that includes tax return information
children.text: An applicant’s FAFSA information that includes return or return information as described in section 6103()(13) of title 26 may be disclosed or redisclosed (which shall include obtaining, sharing, or discussing such information) only in accordance with the procedures described in .lsection 1098h of this title

children.heading: Development of form and information exchange
children.label: 4 Development of form and information exchange
i
children.text: ensure an efficient and effective process;

children.label: ii
children.text: mitigate unintended consequences; and

children.label: iii
children.text: determine the best practices for outreach to students and families during the transition to the streamlined process for the determination of Federal financial aid and Federal Pell Grant eligibility while reducing the data burden on applicants and families; and

children.label: B
children.text: solicit public comments for the format of the free application that provides for adequate time to incorporate feedback prior to development of the application for the succeeding award year.

children.heading: No additional information requests permitted
children.label: 5 No additional information requests permitted
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary may not require additional information to be submitted by an applicant (or the parents or spouse of an applicant) for Federal financial aid through other requirements or reporting, except as required under a process or procedure exercised in accordance with the authority under .section 1087tt of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct outreach to States in order to research the benefits to students of States relying solely on the student aid index, scheduled Pell Grant Award, or the financial data made available, upon authorization by the applicant, as a result of an application for aid under this subsection for determining the eligibility of the applicant for State provided financial aid.

children.label: i
children.text: a list of States that do not require additional financial information separate from the Free Application for Federal Student Aid and do not require asset information from students who qualify for the exemption from asset reporting under for the purposes of awarding State scholarships and grant aid;section 1087ss of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a list of States that require asset information from students who qualify for the exemption from asset reporting under for the purposes of awarding State scholarships and grant aid;section 1087ss of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: a list of States that have indicated that they require additional financial information separate from the Free Application for Federal Student Aid for purposes of awarding State scholarships and grant aid; and

children.label: iv
children.text: with the publication of the lists under this subparagraph, information about additional resources available to applicants, including links to such State websites.

children.label: i
children.text: a list of institutions that do not require additional financial information separate from the Free Application for Federal Student Aid and do not require asset information from students who qualify for the exemption from asset reporting under for the purpose of awarding institution-run financial aid;section 1087ss of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a list of institutions that require asset information from students who qualify for the exemption from asset reporting under for the purpose of awarding institution-run financial aid;section 1087ss of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: a list of institutions that require additional financial information separate from the Free Application for Federal Student Aid for the purpose of awarding institution-run financial aid; and

children.label: iv
children.text: with the publication of the list in clause (iii), information about additional resources available to applicants.

children.label: A
children.text: take all necessary steps to safeguard the data required to be transmitted for the purpose of this section between Federal agencies and to States and institutions of higher education and secure the transmittal of such data;

children.label: B
children.text: provide guidance to States and institutions of higher education regarding their obligation to ensure the security of the data provided under this section and ; andsection 6103 of title 26

children.label: C
children.text: provide guidance on the implementation of , including how it intersects with the provisions of (commonly known as the “Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act of 1974”), and any additional consent processes that may be available to applicants in accordance with title 26 regarding sharing of Federal tax information.section 6103 of title 26section 1232g of this title

children.label: i
children.text: benchmarks for implementation;

children.label: ii
children.text: entities and organizations that the Secretary consulted;

children.label: iii
children.text: system requirements for such implementation and how they will be addressed;

children.label: iv
children.text: any areas of concern and potential problem issues uncovered that may hamper such implementation; and

children.label: v
children.text: solutions determined to address such issues.

children.label: i
children.text: as to the progress and planning described in subparagraph (A) prior to implementation of the revisions to the Free Application for Federal Student Aid under this subsection not less often than quarterly; and

children.label: ii
children.text: at least 6 months and 1 year after implementation of the revisions to the Free Application for Federal Student Aid.

children.label: A
children.text: examples of the special circumstances under which a student or family member may qualify for such adjustment or determination of independence; and

children.label: B
children.text: additional information regarding the steps a student or family member may take in order to seek an adjustment under .section 1087tt of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 9 months after , the Secretary shall begin consumer testing the design of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid under this section with prospective first-generation college students, representatives of students (including low-income students, English learners, first-generation college students, adult students, veterans, servicemembers, and prospective students), students’ families (including low-income families, families with English learners, families with first-generation college students, and families with prospective students), institutions of higher education, secondary school and postsecondary counselors, and nonprofit consumer groups.December 27, 2020

children.heading: Updates
children.label: B Updates
children.text: For award year 2024–2025 and at least each fourth succeeding award year thereafter, the Secretary shall update the design of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid based on additional consumer testing with the populations described in subparagraph (A) in order to improve the usability and accessibility of the application.

children.label: A
children.text: in conjunction with the Bureau of the Census, determine the most common languages spoken by English learner students and their parents in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: develop and make publicly available versions of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid form in not fewer than 11 of the most common languages determined under subparagraph (A) and make such versions available and accessible to applicants in paper and electronic formats; and

children.label: C
children.text: ensure that the Free Application for Federal Student Aid is available in formats accessible to individuals with disabilities and compliant with the most recent Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, or successor guidelines.

children.heading: Reapplication in a succeeding academic year
children.label: 4 Reapplication in a succeeding academic year
children.text: In order to streamline an applicant’s experience in applying for financial aid, the Secretary shall allow an applicant who electronically applies for financial assistance under this subchapter for an academic year subsequent to an academic year for which such applicant applied for financial assistance under this subchapter to automatically electronically import all of the applicant’s (including parents’, guardians’, or spouses’, as applicable) identifying, demographic, and school data from the previous application and to update such information to reflect any circumstances that have changed.

children.label: A
children.text: save data; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit the application under this subchapter to the Secretary through such technology.

children.label: A
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, streamline and simplify the process of verification for applicants for Federal financial aid;

children.label: i
children.text: the burden placed on low-income applicants;

children.label: ii
children.text: the risk to low-income applicants of failing to complete the application, enroll in college, or complete a postsecondary credential as a result of being selected for verification;

children.label: iii
children.text: the effectiveness of the policies and procedures in preventing overpayments; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the reasons for the source of any improper payments; and

children.label: C
children.text: issue a public report not less often than annually that includes the number and percentage of applicants subject to verification, whether the applicants ultimately received Federal financial aid disbursements, the extent to which the student aid index changed for such applicants as a result of verification, and the extent to which such applicants’ eligibility for Federal financial aid under this subchapter changed.

children.label: I
children.text: how much financial aid awards would change if the additional information were not required; and

children.label: II
children.text: the number of students who started but did not finish the Free Application for Federal Student Aid, compared to the baseline year of 2021; and

children.label: I
children.text: started a Free Application for Federal Student Aid but did not receive financial assistance under this subchapter for the applicable academic year; and

children.label: II
children.text: if available, did not enroll in an institution of higher education in the applicable academic year;

children.label: B
children.text: the most common barriers faced by applicants in completing the Free Application for Federal Student Aid; and

children.label: C
children.text: the most common reasons that students and families do not fill out the Free Applications for Federal Student Aid.

children.label: A
children.text: annually on the total number of Free Applications for Federal Student Aid submitted by application cycle, disaggregated by demographic characteristics, type of institution or institutions of higher education to which the applicant applied, the applicant’s State of legal residence, and high school and public school district;

children.label: B
children.text: quarterly on the total number of Free Applications for Federal Student Aid submitted by application cycle, disaggregated by type of institution or institutions of higher education to which the applicant applied, the applicant’s State of legal residence, and high school and public school district;

children.label: C
children.text: weekly on the total number of Free Applications for Federal Student Aid submitted, disaggregated by high school and public school district; and

children.label: i
children.text: an unaccompanied homeless youth or unaccompanied, at risk of homelessness, and self-supporting; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a foster care youth.

children.label: A
children.text: the total number of all applicants who were determined to be individuals described in ; andsection 1087vv(d)(8) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: by State; and

children.label: ii
children.text: by the sources of determination as described in .section 1087uu–2(b) of this title

children.heading: Data sharing
children.label: 3 Data sharing
children.text: The Secretary may enter into data sharing agreements with the appropriate Federal or State agencies to conduct outreach regarding, and connect applicants directly with, the means-tested Federal benefit programs described in subsection (a)(2)(B)(ii)(XVII) for which the applicants may be eligible.

children.heading: Signature
children.label: 1 Signature
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, the Secretary may permit the Free Application for Federal Student Aid to be submitted without a signature, if a signature is subsequently submitted by the applicant, or if the applicant uses an access device provided by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: includes, at the time the application is submitted to the Department, the name, address or employer’s address, social security number or employer identification number, and organizational affiliation of the preparer on the applicant’s form;

children.label: B
children.text: is subject to the same penalties as an applicant for purposely giving false or misleading information in the application;

children.label: C
children.text: clearly informs each individual upon initial contact, that the Free Application for Federal Student Aid is a free form that may be completed without professional assistance; and

children.label: D
children.text: does not produce, use, or disseminate any other form for the purpose of applying for Federal financial aid other than the Free Application for Federal Student Aid developed by the Secretary under this section.

children.heading: Charges to students and parents for use of forms prohibited
children.label: 3 Charges to students and parents for use of forms prohibited
children.text: The need for and eligibility of a student for financial assistance under this subchapter may be determined only by using the Free Application for Federal Student Aid developed by the Secretary under this section. Such application shall be produced, distributed, and processed by the Secretary, and no parent or student shall be charged a fee by the Secretary, a contractor, a third-party servicer or private software provider, or any other public or private entity for the collection, processing, or delivery of Federal financial aid through the use of such application. No data collected on a form for which a fee is charged shall be used to complete the Free Application for Federal Student Aid prescribed under this section, except that a Federal or State income tax form prepared by a paid income tax preparer or preparer service for the primary purpose of filing a Federal or State income tax return may be used to complete the Free Application for Federal Student Aid prescribed under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall enable applicants to submit a Free Application for Federal Student Aid developed under this section and initiate the processing of such application, not later than October 1 prior to the applicant’s planned year of enrollment.

children.label: I
children.text: certify to the authorizing committees that the Department will meet the October 1 deadline specified in subparagraph (A) for such processing cycle; or

children.label: II
children.text: certify to such committees that the Department will not meet such deadline; and

children.label: I
children.text: the anticipated failure to meet such deadline; and

children.label: II
children.text: the financial impact such failure will have on students and families.

children.heading: Early estimates
children.label: 5 Early estimates
children.text: The Secretary shall maintain an electronic method for applicants to enter income and family size information to calculate a non-binding estimate of the applicant’s Federal financial aid available under this subchapter and shall place such calculator on a prominent location at the beginning of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid.

children.heading: Additional forms
children.label: 6 Additional forms
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, an institution may not condition the packaging or receipt of Federal financial aid on the completion of additional requests for financial information beyond the Free Application for Federal Student Aid, unless such information is required for verification, a determination of independence, or professional judgement.
